acceptance_targets: [chapter_05_award_01] Pass if the revised material replacing snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:265, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:279, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:411 contains only one explicit verbalization that the money has changed the relationship, and the post-transfer beat shows the shift through action or silence before any abstract summary. Fail if two or more lines still paraphrase the same idea in thesis language.

[FA-004] Pass if those three moments now read as differentiated reactions and no more than one 'almost [verb]' construction remains across the cited lines. Fail if the chapter still stacks multiple near-identical hesitation beats in those spots.
[FA-014] Pass if both cited lines now show June's response through specific behavior and neither uses unreadable, blank, or flat language. Fail if either line still labels her face that way.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material replacing snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:265, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:279, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:411 contains only one explicit verbalization that the money has changed the relationship, and the post-transfer beat shows the shift through action or silence before any abstract summary. Fail if two or more lines still paraphrase the same idea in thesis language.
findings.problem: The June scene keeps restating the chapter's thesis after the scene has already made it legible: the money will now be 'in the room,' Lena must not pretend her 'thumb slipped,' the transfer is 'real,' and the green check mark must be remembered if Friday 'gets cute.' That repeated naming of the same moral turn over-manages reader interpretation and slightly blunts the queasy inevitability the chapter has otherwise earned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the debate and immediate aftermath around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:265, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:279, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:411 so the relationship shift is stated directly only once. Replace at least two of the thesis-forward lines with observable pressure: changed handling of the phone, altered eye contact, silence, or a more materially awkward departure that lets the transfer's new leverage register without being paraphrased for the reader.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if those three moments now read as differentiated reactions and no more than one 'almost [verb]' construction remains across the cited lines. Fail if the chapter still stacks multiple near-identical hesitation beats in those spots.
findings.problem: The full-book 'almost [verb]' pattern appears in 9 chapters, and chapter 5 stacks three versions of it at lines 2373, 2683, and 2701. Three distinct emotional beats end up sounding like one repeated sentence habit.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2373, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2683, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2701, replace at least two of the three 'almost smiled' or 'almost cried' beats with scene-specific physical or verbal reactions. Preserve the progression from irritation to rueful relief, but make each turn legible through different behavior rather than the same withheld-response syntax.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines now show June's response through specific behavior and neither uses unreadable, blank, or flat language. Fail if either line still labels her face that way.
findings.problem: The novel's unreadable-face pattern appears in 9 chapters, and chapter 5 uses it twice: 'unreadable already' at line 2471 and 'face went blank again' at line 2607. The chapter explains the default so explicitly that it starts to feel habitual instead of observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2471 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2607 so June's skepticism and fatigue register through question rhythm, eye movement, or the speed of her reply instead of unreadable or blank-face shorthand. This chapter already has enough pressure without leaning on the stock face-shutdown signal.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
